build_sequence: n2-attestation (RED entry: new assertIncludes needles in the 5 contract validators + new walkthrough fn testAttestationWarningPersistence beside scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:14940; run `node scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js` + the walkthrough to see RED) → n3-sink-journal (RED entry: flip the asserts at scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:14488 and :14503-14504 in testSinkTransactionCleanEndToEnd to journal-ABSENT; RED until sink-merge deletes at terminal success) → n4-candidate-binding (RED entry: new consumer finalize-check walkthrough cases beside scripts/simulate-workflow-walkthrough.js:3964-4058 asserting final_validation_unbound / final_validation_stale; RED until gate + producer mode + fixture hashes land) → n5-selection-rungaps (RED entry: new case in scripts/test-gap-sweep.js — a summary-observed ## Run gaps entry with empty sweptClasses must refuse observed_gap_unseeded, currently passes vacuously at gap-sweep.js:254-264; plus walkthrough assert closure_receipt.selection_evidence === 'present' near :8633) → n6-claim-ports (no natural RED — mechanical mirror of `git diff <run-base>..HEAD -- scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js` into both forge claim ports; forge-walkthrough attestation-persistence + selection-evidence asserts authored HERE are the behavioral gate, extending the existing blocks at gitlab walkthrough:1017-1036 / gitea:1282-1301) → n7/n8/n9/n10 per plan.

---

### A. Planner attestation (n2)

**Verified anchors.** checkDispatchAttestations claim.js:79-122 (fields claim_planner_attested / finalize_contractor_attested; pushes 'ATTESTATION WARNING: no workflow-planner dispatch found in dispatch-log — claim/author seam may have been run inline by main session' at :117). Planner back-fill claimProject:1058-1074 (single-issue), bundle mirror :1257-1267, honored via attestPlannerSpawn passthrough at :1454. Contractor back-fill cmdFinalize:2550-2564. The check runs at :2565 — after archiveProjectDir (:2246) but BEFORE appendClosureBlock (:2580), so attestation results are in scope when the durable archive records are written. appendClosureBlock (:1734-1748) appends `## Closure` to workflow-state.md, presence-guarded on /^## Closure$/m. The #324 sanitizer (:1882-1910) runs earlier inside archiveProjectDir and only rewrites two known sentinels — no interaction.

**A1 — TOML trio text** (edit instruction `1.` on line 22, identically in all three workflow-planner.toml; trio must stay byte-identical). After "This creates the worktree + workflow-state.md.", insert:

"Always pass --attest-planner-spawn on this startup call (single-issue AND bundle): it back-fills your own otherwise-unloggable dispatch marker into .cache/dispatch-log.jsonl so the closure receipt can record claim_planner_attested: attested. Only a genuinely-dispatched workflow-planner running this startup passes it; an inline main-session run must not."

**A2 — adapt SKILL ×3** (edition script names: kaola-workflow-claim.js / kaola-gitlab-workflow-claim.js / kaola-gitea-workflow-claim.js). Two touch points each:
- Line 184-185 single-issue call: extend the backticked command to `<claim-script> startup --runtime <runtime> --workflow-path adaptive --target-issue <issue> --attest-planner-spawn` and add to the parenthetical: "--attest-planner-spawn is REQUIRED on every planner-run startup — it back-fills the planner's own dispatch marker into .cache/dispatch-log.jsonl for closure attestation; only the dispatched workflow-planner passes it".
- Bundle startup block (:238-246): add line `  --attest-planner-spawn \` between `--target-issues 42,47,53 \` and `$KAOLA_DISPATCH_MODE_FLAG` (bundle path honors it — claim.js:1454).

agents/workflow-planner.md:294 already carries the flag — NO edit.

**A3 — warning persistence (claim.js root + codex byte-copy).** New helper persistAttestationToSummary(destDir, receipt) beside appendClosureBlock (:1734): presence-guarded on /^## Attestation$/m, swallow-on-error, appends to destDir/finalization-summary.md (create-if-absent):

```
## Attestation
claim_planner_attested: <value>
finalize_contractor_attested: <value>
<every receipt.warnings entry starting with 'ATTESTATION WARNING' or 'attestation:', verbatim, one per line>
```

Always write the two column-0 status fields, even when both are attested — a clean result is a positive statement, not an absence. Call site in cmdFinalize immediately after checkDispatchAttestations(:2565), before computeGoalCheck(:2568): `if (result.dest) persistAttestationToSummary(result.dest, closureReceipt);`. Lands inside the keep-worktree `chore: archive` commit (commit block is at the END, :2590-2628). Also extend appendClosureBlock with claim_planner_attested / finalize_contractor_attested lines so workflow-state.md's terminal receipt is self-contained.

**A4 — contract-validator needles (exact new asserts):**
- scripts/validate-kaola-workflow-contracts.js (codex chain, near :604): `assertIncludes(pluginRoot + '/agents/workflow-planner.toml', '--attest-planner-spawn');` and `assertIncludes(pluginRoot + '/skills/kaola-workflow-adapt/SKILL.md', '--attest-planner-spawn');`
- gitlab validator (near :740) and gitea validator (near :745): same pair with `pluginRoot + '/agents/workflow-planner.toml'` / `pluginRoot + '/skills/kaola-workflow-adapt/SKILL.md'`.
- scripts/validate-workflow-contracts.js + codex byte-copy (claude chain, near :721): `assertIncludes('agents/workflow-planner.md', '--attest-planner-spawn');` and persistence lock `assertIncludes('scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js', '## Attestation');`

**A5 — finalize prose** (command :818-833 region + Summary File sections; finalize SKILLs ×3 near :455-480; contractor.md + contractor.toml ×3), one provenance-free rule: transcribe any non-empty attestation warning verbatim into finalization-summary.md; never report an unqualified clean delegation while an ATTESTATION WARNING exists — the finalize script also appends a `## Attestation` section to the archived summary; do not remove or summarize it.

**A6 — RED test.** Walkthrough new fn testAttestationWarningPersistence beside :14940: claim a project, seed .cache/dispatch-log.jsonl with ONLY a contractor entry, run finalize, assert archived finalization-summary.md contains column-0 `claim_planner_attested: missing` and verbatim substring 'ATTESTATION WARNING: no workflow-planner dispatch found in dispatch-log', and the archived workflow-state.md ## Closure block carries the attestation lines. Mirror compactly in the codex walkthrough beside its AC3 block (:221-261). RED today: cmdFinalize emits the warning only on stdout JSON.

### B. Terminal sink-journal lifecycle (n3)

**Verified anchors.** #520 staging exclusion sink-merge.js:1292-1298, commit-side :1315; SINK_STEPS :788 (closure last); writeSinkReceipt :791-796; resolveSinkReceiptPath :798-808 (live then archive); #484 freshness guard :1507-1532; teardown :1534-1551; success emit :1553-1557 — finalReceipt is READ FROM DISK INTO MEMORY at :1554-1556 before emit; that is the deletion hook point.

**B1 — delete-on-terminal-success.** New helper disposeSinkJournals(mainRoot, project): unlink sink-receipt.json AND sink-fallback.json from BOTH kaola-workflow/<project>/.cache/ and kaola-workflow/archive/<project>/.cache/ (4 candidate paths, per-file try/catch; a failed unlink warns on stderr and must never fail a successful sink). Call at :1556+, after finalReceipt is parsed, immediately before the success emit; extend the emit to `{ result:'ok', status:'sinked', journal_disposed: true|false, receipt: finalReceipt }`. Crash-resume untouched by construction: deletion sits strictly after all steps done + the #484 ancestry guard + teardown, so any earlier crash keeps the journal (existing abort test at walkthrough:14305-14336 stays green unchanged). Re-run-after-success note for n8: today an all-done lingering receipt + deleted branch refuses stale_sink_receipt; after deletion a re-run refuses at preflight/branch resolution — a refusal either way, no green-path regression. sink-pr's fallback read (sink-pr.js:201-205) is the PR lane, out of scope; at direct-sink terminal success the fallback journal is stale by definition, so deleting both is correct. NO claim.js hunk — keeps n6's mirror smaller.

**B2 — forge sink-merge ports.** kaola-gitlab-workflow-sink-merge.js / kaola-gitea-workflow-sink-merge.js are DIVERGENT hand-ports — mirror the deletion hunk + emit field manually IN n3 (they are in n3's declared set; NOT n6's, which is claim.js-only). Behavior asserts in test-gitlab-sinks.js / test-gitea-sinks.js.

**B3 — prose fix** (the "clean and synced" trap). Surfaces: finalize SKILL ×3 (the `| final commit and push | invoked | git status --short --branch | clean and synced |` row at :479 — add a note under the table), finalize command (crash-resume para :951, post-sink confirm :955), plan-run command + SKILLs ×3 (All-done/sink flow). Rule text (provenance-free): "sink-receipt.json / sink-fallback.json are transaction journals owned by the sink script — they exist on disk only for crash-resume, and a terminally successful sink deletes them itself. A 'clean and synced' check that finds one afterwards (an older cycle's residue) must DELETE the file, never commit it; a journal is never part of the deliverable."

**B4 — RED test.** Flip testSinkTransactionCleanEndToEnd: at :14484-14492 assert all-steps-done from the STDOUT receipt (JSON.parse(result.stdout).receipt.steps) instead of the on-disk file; replace :14488 and :14503-14504 with asserts that NEITHER archiveReceiptPath NOR liveReceiptPath exists and journal_disposed === true; keep the :14497-14501 git ls-files empty assert; add `git status --porcelain` contains no sink-receipt/sink-fallback path.

### C. validated_candidate_hash binding (n4)

**Verified anchors.** Consumer gate arm PV:3354-3368 (final_validation_unverified :3359-3361, final_validation_failed :3363-3366, schema.parseNodeVerdict :3363); computeCodeTreeHash(root, project, testConsumedExtra) PV:2364-2377 (landable snapshot → ls-tree -r → drop isValidationInvisible → sorted sha256; null on git failure = fail-closed); isValidationInvisible PV:321-328; parseValidationTestConsumes PV:502-507 (Meta-scoped, hash-covered); projTag PV:2782; hint table PV:107-108; --current-code-tree test seam PV:3319. cmdFinalize ALREADY verifies before any side effect: the #522 gate (claim.js:2208-2245) shells --finalize-check before archiveProjectDir (:2246) — so C needs ZERO claim.js change; the binding lives entirely in the GENERATED plan-validator (auto-propagates via sync:editions) + byte-identical adaptive-schema.

**C1 — field + parser.** Column-0 `validated_candidate_hash: <64-hex-lowercase>` in .cache/final-validation.md. Parser in adaptive-schema beside parseNodeVerdict (:527-539), same pure/fence-blind/last-match-wins discipline: parseValidatedCandidateHash(text) → { present, hash } where present = any column-0 `^validated_candidate_hash:` line exists (gm) and hash = last well-formed `^validated_candidate_hash:[ \t]*([0-9a-fA-F]{64})[ \t]*$` match lowercased, else null.

**C2 — producer CLI.** New plan-validator mode `--candidate-hash [--json]` beside --finalize-check (:3224): resolve hashRoot via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` exactly as :3317-3318, extra = parseValidationTestConsumes(content) — the frozen plan is the shared band source (consumer mode has no receipt to replay; the plan is freeze/hash-covered, so producer and gate derive the identical band by construction) — emit `{ result:'ok', mode:'candidate-hash', validated_candidate_hash: computeCodeTreeHash(hashRoot, projTag, extra) }`; null hash → typed refuse `candidate_hash_unavailable`, exit 1.

**C3 — gate.** In the consumer arm after the verdict check (:3367): parse via schema.parseValidatedCandidateHash(fvRaw); !present || !hash → typed refuse **final_validation_unbound** (fail-closed — an omitted field must not bypass the binding); else recompute current = flagVal('--current-code-tree') || computeCodeTreeHash(hashRoot, projTag, parseValidationTestConsumes(content)); !current || current !== hash → typed refuse **final_validation_stale** with payload fields `recorded_candidate_hash` + `current_candidate_hash`; match → the pass payload (:3395) gains `validated_candidate_hash`. Precedence (extend comment :3235): final_validation_unverified > final_validation_failed > final_validation_unbound > final_validation_stale. Two new operator hints beside PV:107-108: unbound → "final-validation.md lacks a column-0 validated_candidate_hash — recompute via --candidate-hash --json and re-record after confirming the tree still matches the validated candidate; if uncertain, re-run the validation command"; stale → "a relevant source/test/test-consumed file changed after validation — re-run the recorded validation_command and re-record final-validation.md (including a fresh hash); never hand-patch the hash". No test execution anywhere in the gate — it compares two hashes; self-host chain-receipt arm and the citation fields stay byte-untouched in decision terms.

**C4 — prose** (plan-run All-done consumer block command :448-456 + De-Dup :476-482, SKILL mirrors ×3 at :501+; finalize command consumer para :140-150 + finalize SKILLs ×3; contractor ×4 remedy line): after recording verdict: pass (+ citation fields where citing), run `node "$KAOLA_SCRIPTS/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js" kaola-workflow/{project}/workflow-plan.md --candidate-hash --json` and record the value as a column-0 validated_candidate_hash: line — compute it LAST, after every file the validation covered has landed; any later relevant edit stales the binding at finalize. Contractor: on final_validation_stale, re-run + re-record; never patch the hash. Needles: 'validated_candidate_hash' asserted in the plan-run SKILL + finalize SKILL by each codex-edition validator, and in commands/kaola-workflow-plan-run.md by validate-workflow-contracts.js.

**C5 — RED tests** (walkthrough consumer cases beside :3964-4058; update the existing consumer + citation fixtures there to carry a valid hash — they refuse final_validation_unbound otherwise, which is the intended RED): (a) pass-verdict, no hash → final_validation_unbound; (b) hash recorded, then touch a code file → final_validation_stale with both hash fields; (c) hash matches → result:'pass' + echoed validated_candidate_hash; (d) hash recorded, then a validation-invisible edit (workflow-state / inert doc) → still pass; (e) --candidate-hash emits 64-hex equal to the gate's recompute. Use the existing --current-code-tree seam for determinism. Keep parser asserts inside these walkthrough cases — do NOT add a new unit-test file (write-set discipline).

### D. Selection evidence + run-gap seeding (n5)

**Verified anchors.** gap-sweep.js: scanManual :112-140 (seed grammar `gap: <class> — <text>` in .cache/run-gaps-manual.md), vacuous pass :254-264 (returns before ever reading the summary — the observed hole), parseGapSection :195-229 (strict entry regex :216; non-matching lines like `- none` ignored → back-compat safe), forward-only matching :288-303. CLOSURE_RECEIPT_FIELDS closure-contract.js:20-59 (×4 byte-identical), goal_check-style null default :100-101. workflow-next scout dispatch :140-193 (recommendation lives only in the scout's reply today; no 'selection-evidence' string exists anywhere in the repo — new artifact).

**D1 — gap-sweep reverse containment.** In runCheck, parse the summary ## Run gaps section FIRST (even when sweptClasses is empty); every strict-regex entry must exist in sweptClasses as an exact (reasonClass, sample) tuple; violators → typed refuse **observed_gap_unseeded** with `unseeded: [{reasonClass, sample}]` + detail: seed via .cache/run-gaps-manual.md (`gap: <class> — <text>`), re-run the scanner, then --check. Then the existing forward check (gaps_unswept) unchanged. Vacuous pass only when BOTH sides are empty. Apply the identical edit to all four gap-sweep files in n5 (codex byte-pair + the two rename-normalized forge ports — validate-script-sync.js red-flags a lag in every chain).

**D2 — selection-evidence docking (prose-only mechanism).** commands/workflow-next.md after Output→env wiring (:177-193) + next SKILLs ×3: on the no-issue-named branch, once startup returns {project}, the router writes the scout's ENTIRE JSON reply verbatim (fenced) to kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/selection-evidence.md with a one-line header `selection_mode: auto-bundle|single-issue` — before dispatching the executor; it archives with the cycle automatically. agents/issue-scout.md + issue-scout.toml ×3 get one line: the router persists your JSON output as the durable selection evidence — return it complete and valid.

**D3 — receipt field.** Add `selection_evidence: ['present', 'absent']` to CLOSURE_RECEIPT_FIELDS (+ goal_check-style null default in emptyReceipt) ×4 copies; in cmdFinalize (claim.js root + codex byte-copy) compute beside the attestation probe (:2548-2565): any file matching selection-evidence.* in archiveCacheDir || liveCacheDir → 'present', else 'absent'; attach like probe_degraded (:2504). Advisory only — NO invariant, NO warning on absence (a user-named claim legitimately has none).

**D4 — seeding prose.** Finalize command Run-Gap Sweep Gate (:152-171) + finalize SKILLs ×3 (:124): add the observed_gap_unseeded refusal row + rule; plan-run command + SKILLs ×3: when the orchestrator observes a run gap the scanners cannot see (transient tool noise, manual retry, environmental flake), append a `gap: <class> — <text>` line to kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/run-gaps-manual.md immediately — BEFORE the sweep — so the mapping to noise:/filed: is machine-checked, never vacuous. Needles: 'selection-evidence' (workflow-next + next SKILLs), 'observed_gap_unseeded' (finalize command + SKILLs), 'run-gaps-manual.md' (plan-run surfaces) across the 5 validators.

**D5 — RED tests.** scripts/test-gap-sweep.js: (1) summary has `- manual:coresim-busy (one transient Busy event): noise: environment` with sweptClasses: [] → --check refuses observed_gap_unseeded (passes vacuously today); (2) same gap seeded through run-gaps-manual.md → scanner emits manual:coresim-busy → --check passes mapped:1, noise:1; (3) forward direction still refuses gaps_unswept. Walkthrough near :8633: seed .cache/selection-evidence.md pre-finalize → closure_receipt.selection_evidence === 'present' + file exists under archive dest; absent case → 'absent'.

### n6 isolation & assumptions

- claim.js hunks by node (what n6 mirrors): n2 = persistAttestationToSummary + call + appendClosureBlock field extension; n5 = selection_evidence probe/attach. n3 and n4 contribute ZERO claim.js hunks if this spec is followed — state this in each node's evidence so the accumulated diff stays cleanly attributable.
- NO write-set repair needed — everything fits the frozen declared sets. Declared-but-expected-untouched: scripts/test-route-reachability.js in n2–n5 (verified :1-80 — it checks route-target existence, not concept needles; routes don't change), scripts/kaola-workflow-claim.js in n3/n4, scripts/test-claim-hardening.js in n3 (walkthrough is the natural home for the flip). Do NOT add new test files (e.g. a schema-parser unit) — that would overflow the declared sets.
- Byte/rename discipline per node: claim.js, sink-merge.js, gap-sweep.js, validate-workflow-contracts.js root↔codex byte-pairs must move together in the same node; gap-sweep gitlab/gitea rename-normalized ports too; plan-validator/adaptive-schema propagate via `npm run sync:editions` (never hand-edit forge ports); the workflow-planner.toml / contractor.toml / issue-scout.toml trios and closure-contract ×4 stay byte-identical.
- Provenance rule: none of the TOML/SKILL/command/agent edits may carry issue refs — all needles above are provenance-free rule text.
- Expected chain posture mid-run: claude+codex chains green per node; gitlab/gitea chains fully green only from n6 (forge claim ports lag by design, per Plan Notes).

New typed refusal names introduced: final_validation_unbound, final_validation_stale, candidate_hash_unavailable (producer), observed_gap_unseeded. New receipt/payload fields: validated_candidate_hash (pass payload + final-validation.md), recorded_candidate_hash / current_candidate_hash (stale refusal), journal_disposed (sink emit), selection_evidence (closure receipt). New flag/CLI surface: --candidate-hash (plan-validator). New durable artifacts: ## Attestation section in archived finalization-summary.md; .cache/selection-evidence.md.
